questions.question: Describe the difference between specifying a NOT NULL constraint as an assertion and as an action trigger in a database management system. Provide an example of each. (15 marks)
A database table has a column named 'age' with a NOT NULL constraint specified as an action trigger. What happens when a user inserts a NULL value into this column? (5 marks)

A NOT NULL constraint is specified as an assertion for the 'name' column in a database table. A trigger is also defined to check if the 'name' column contains duplicate values. What is the primary purpose of the trigger in this scenario? (10 marks)
